Pricing · Missive
Your Privacy Choices
We and our vendors use technology that collects data about your use of our site so we can improve and personalize our products and services, for analytics and marketing, and to fulfill your requests. We may also share this information with marketing vendors, social media companies, and analytics partners. By using our website, you acknowledge and agree to our Terms of Use.
To learn more about our privacy practices review our
Privacy Policy
.

Live sessions with our experts
Frequently asked questions
Can I try Missive first?
Yes! Everyone starts on Missive’s free plan so they can test out features and functionality, to make sure there is a good fit. No credit card is required.
Can I import more than one account?
Yes, Missive supports connecting multiple accounts (email, SMS, WhatsApp, etc.). Each user can have up to 10 personal accounts and 5 shared accounts. For example, an organization with 10 users can have up to 50 shared accounts, and an organization with 100 users can have up to 500 shared accounts. Additional shared accounts can be purchased.
Will my personal emails be shared with my coworkers?
No, your coworkers will never see your personal emails by default. You can choose to grant them access to your account or invite them on a per-conversation basis.
What devices do you support?
Missive is primarily a web app, accessible from any web browser.
We also offer mobile apps (iOS and Android) and desktop apps (macOS and Windows). Visit our
download page.
Can you help migrate my existing system?
If you're migrating from Google Workspace or Outlook, the process will be straightforward — see
here
. For migrations from platforms like Front, we've created a simple guide, which you can find
here
.
Our team is always happy to assist and ensure you get the most out of Missive. Don’t hesitate to reach out!
Can I add or remove users later?
Yes — Admins can add more users to the team. You can also remove individual team users from your plan through Admin controls. For Yearly plans, you will continue to pay for the removed user until the end of the billing period, but if you add a new team member, the seat will be re-assigned to them.
What happens if we cancel our subscription?
After canceling your plan, you will retain access to all features until the end of your current billing cycle. Once the billing cycle ends, your content will remain available in read-only mode.
Still have more questions?
We would love to help — Visit
our help center
or simply
email us.
We also
host a bi-weekly demo
where we share tips and tricks for getting the most out of Missive and discover how it can help improve your team collaboration and communication.

Changelog
What’s new in Missive?
11.30.1
June 19, 2026
New
Share links to labels and mailboxes in comments
You can now copy a shareable link to any label or mailbox and paste it into a comment. The link renders inline as a clickable token using the label or mailbox’s display name, so teammates can jump to the right view without needing directions. This helps when:
Pointing a colleague to the exact label where a customer thread lives
Linking to a team inbox in a handoff note
Inline signature switcher in the composer
A new "Signature" button now appears in the top-right corner of the signature area when you hover over it or place your cursor inside. Choose a different signature without changing the From address. A dashed outline around the signature also makes it clear when you're editing that area, helping prevent accidental changes.
Update conversations through the API without posting a comment
A new endpoint on the public REST API lets you close, reopen, assign, label, recolor, rename, or move conversations without creating a post in the thread. Useful when you want to automate housekeeping from your own tools, sync state from a CRM, or trigger label-change rules from a script, all without cluttering the conversation with system comments.
‍
Improved
The Tasks calendar overlay now only shows tasks assigned to you, matching the task list view instead of every task you can access.
Drag and drop on iPad with a connected mouse now works the same as on desktop, while touch gestures keep their default scroll and swipe behavior.
Calendar sync now only fetches events from the calendars you have visible, cutting unnecessary requests when you've hidden calendars from view.
Label menus search and display more reliably, including better handling of nested labels and label autocomplete with
#
Fixed
Switching the From alias on a forward no longer repopulates the empty To field with the original message's recipients.
Drag and drop of file attachments into the composer now works when the cursor is over an HTML signature with a table.
Calendar reminders no longer fire for events you've declined.
A phantom unread badge on the mobile menu and dock could stick around with no clearable source if you'd hidden calendar reminders from the Activity feed. The badge now matches what's actually visible.
The new conversation title field no longer loses focus right after the conversation is created.
The contact editor now warns about unsaved changes when you click outside it or switch to another contact book.
Switching an account's sharing between organizations no longer requires making it private first.
AI-generated drafts now properly collapse the quoted reply, so you don't have to scroll past it to reach the edit or send button on mobile.
The calendar color picker no longer crashes when opened on a calendar you don't manage; it's now disabled with a clear indicator.
Pressing Escape on an unsaved rule then Enter now confirms the cancel action, matching the keyboard behavior of other dialogs.
AI prompts run on text selected inside a quoted reply now receive the actual selected text instead of coming back empty.
Copying from a context menu in Firefox and Safari no longer writes to the clipboard twice or surfaces a spurious error.
Typing inside a signature with a table layout no longer causes the cursor to jump.
11.29.1
June 6, 2026
New
Improved
Fixed
Logging in with an OAuth provider on the desktop and mobile apps since the previous version.
11.29.0
June 4, 2026
New
Set your default meeting length
‍
If your team runs 25 or 50-minute "speedy meetings," you no longer have to adjust every new event manually. Set your preferred duration once in
Settings → Preferences → Calendars
, and Missive will use it from then on.
Improved shared address management
‍
Shared addresses (Google Groups, distribution lists, etc.) now live in their own section under
Settings → Accounts
. A new guided setup flow makes it easier to add them and configure how incoming emails get routed.
Copy attachments with a right-click
‍
Right-click any attachment to copy it straight to your clipboard. No more opening a preview just to grab a file! Works for all file types on the app; PNG images in the browser.
‍
Improved
Rule actions: Smarter AI model selection
‍
AI actions now show all available models in two clear categories: Fast (runs immediately, can trigger follow-up rules) and Powerful (better results, runs in the background). The list stays in sync with your AI integration settings automatically.
Better Markdown rendering in comments
‍
Missive now supports CommonMark, the standard behind GitHub, Notion, and many modern tools. Bold, italic, and strikethrough formatting using double delimiters (
**like this**
) displays exactly as intended.
Emoji-only status
‍
Set a quick status with an emoji, no text required! A 🌴 or 🎧 now speaks for itself.
More reliable AI assistant for large tool call
‍
When the AI handles a lot at once, like searching across multiple accounts or making bulk changes, it now processes results in smaller batches. Fewer errors, smoother long sessions
A proper dialog to import contacts from CSV
‍
A new dialog walks you through CSV imports with instructions, drag-and-drop and downloadable templates for Google and Outlook formats. No more guessing why your file didn't work.
Redesigned contact book creation and editing
‍
Creating and editing contact books got a more consistent design, with the ability to import contacts from CSV right after.
‍
Fixed
Reopening a minimized composer window on the desktop app now correctly restores and focuses the window instead of leaving it hidden.
Dismissing a calendar reminder now properly updates the app badge count on your device.
Search no longer fails for organizations with many connected accounts when the default excluded-account filters exceed the previous limit.
"Create draft with AI" rule action now correctly uses the sender alias selected in the rule configuration, instead of falling back to the triggering user's default alias.
Reply drafts created by the AI assistant now include the quoted previous message, matching the behavior of manually composed replies.
Pasting a table copied from an external source (e.g. a spreadsheet or webpage) into the composer now renders cleanly instead of inserting raw HTML.
Copying text or email addresses on iOS no longer shows a "Couldn't copy to clipboard" error. The copy still worked, but the false error was confusing.
Custom channel account avatars are now displayed correctly instead of showing a broken image placeholder.
Cloud file attachment icons (Google Drive, Dropbox, etc.) in comment metadata now display properly.
Long status text no longer gets cut off in the UI.
Calendar events in the sidebar no longer overlap the conversation tile.
Dialpad follow-up events (like transcriptions) for team-routed calls no longer resurface the conversation in individual users' personal inboxes.
11.27.1
May 21, 2026
New
Improved
Fixed
Changing the From alias in the composer not working properly (regression from 11.27.0)
11.27.0
May 21, 2026
New
Duplicate rules to a different scope
‍
Until now, if you built a useful personal rule and wanted to share it with your organization, you had to export the rule and re-import it manually. The Duplicate action now opens a sub-menu that lets you pick a destination: Personal, the rule's current organization, or any other organization you manage. Missive creates a pre-filled copy scoped to that target, automatically removing any conditions or actions that don't apply to the new scope. Much faster than export/import when you want to reuse a rule across contexts.
Improved
Improved MCP integrations in the assistant
‍
MCP integrations are now loaded on demand instead of all up front, so connecting several integrations no longer burns tokens on capabilities the assistant never uses. Handling of multiple MCP integrations of the same type (e.g. two Stripe accounts) has also been improved so they stay distinct.
Instant calendar loading
Your calendar events are now saved on your device, so they show up immediately when you open or reload Missive. No more blank calendar while things load, fresh updates sync quietly in the background.
Out of office unassignment is now visible in conversations
‍
When a teammate's Out of office status causes them to be unassigned from a conversation, that action now appears as a visible event in the conversation timeline, so the rest of the team can see why the conversation returned to the team inbox.
Conversation subjects preserved from out-of-office replies
‍
Receiving an out-of-office auto-reply no longer overwrites the conversation's displayed subject line with the OOO message subject.
Mailto links now support the
from
parameter
‍
When a
mailto:
link includes a
from
field (e.g.
mailto:john@acme.com?from=sales@acme.com
), Missive automatically selects the matching sender alias. Useful for CRMs, help desks, or internal tools that generate mailto links where the sending address matters.
Account provider icon badges in the sidebar
‍
Per-account sub-mailboxes under your unified Inbox and All now show a small provider icon badge (e.g. Messenger, Aircall, Dialpad), so you can distinguish between accounts that share the same name. Only non-email accounts get a badge. You can turn this off under Settings > Preferences > Left sidebar.
Fixed freeze when opening drafts with very long canned responses
‍
Indexing canned responses could cause a multi-second freeze for organizations with large response libraries. This has been resolved.
Better email quote detection for dashed separators
‍
Quoted text detection has been improved for messages that use dashed separators (common in forwarded emails from HEY and other providers). Quoted text is now correctly collapsed in more cases.
‍
Fixed
Search results now show the latest message preview and organization labels again, instead of the "N matches" count that replaced them in the previous release.
Calendar reminders no longer fire for events that were rescheduled or deleted. Previously, moving an event could leave behind a stale reminder that would still notify you at the original time.
Accepting a calendar invite that includes multiple team members now correctly RSVPs for the right person, instead of sometimes targeting the wrong attendee.
The "Auto-shared users" dropdown in the organization label editor now lists all teams in your organization, not just teams with a team inbox.
Triple-backtick code blocks in comments now render correctly when the code inside contains backticks (e.g. template literals or shell commands). Previously, the inner backticks broke the formatting.
Image URLs with query parameters (e.g. Dropbox share links with auth tokens) pasted in comments are no longer stripped of their query string, which could break the link or prevent the image from loading.
Scrolling to the bottom of search results no longer causes the jump-between-matches navigation bar to overlap conversation content.
Copying text to the clipboard now shows a clear error dialog when the browser blocks the operation, instead of failing silently.
Declining a single occurrence of a Microsoft recurring calendar event no longer declines the entire series.
Dialpad forwarded calls to a shared number now correctly land in the shared team inbox instead of the operator's personal inbox.
Email account deletion no longer gets stuck in a "being deleted" state indefinitely.
Organization-owned Shopify integrations can now be reconnected after they disconnect.
MCP tools no longer fail to load on Gemini when using multiple integrations from the same MCP server.
Long-pressing a file attachment on mobile no longer triggers overlapping context menus.
mailto:
links in comments now preserve query string parameters.
‍
11.26.0
May 8, 2026
New
Search: Jump directly to matching results
‍
Search results now open directly on the matching message or email, instead of dropping you at the bottom of the conversation. All matching entries are highlighted, and you can jump between them with navigation arrows. No more scrolling through long threads to find what you’re looking for.
Assistant can now manage labels and calendar events
‍
Missive AI Assistant can now apply, update, or remove labels from conversations, and create, update, or delete calendar events on your behalf. You’ll be asked to approve actions before anything happens.
From this newsletter, create all next month’s events in my calendar.
Review all conversations in the Prospect label. If Stripe shows the company has upgraded, add the Customer label and remove Prospect.
Find conversations labeled Billing that mention a meeting date, then create calenda

GitBook Assistant
Ask
On this page
Get started
What is Missive?
Missive is the collaborative email client built for teams. Manage email, SMS, WhatsApp, and more in one shared inbox.
Missive is the collaborative email client built for teams. Work together on shared inboxes, discuss emails internally, and respond across email, SMS, WhatsApp, and more from one place.
How teams use Missive
Share email accounts with your team without forwarding, CC'ing, or sharing passwords
Add comments and
@mentions
to any conversation (visible only to your team)
Co-write drafts in real-time with colleagues
Manage email, SMS, WhatsApp, Instagram, Messenger, and live chat in one inbox
Automate repetitive work with rules and AI
Who uses Missive
Customer support teams, sales teams, and agencies. Any business where email is mission-critical and collaboration matters.
Join a live session
We run live 45-minute sessions twice a week covering team inboxes, collaboration features, and Q&A with the Missive team.
Register here
.
Video library
Tutorials, deep dives, customer stories, and workflow ideas.
Watch videos
.

integrations.title: Integrations · Missive
integrations.text:
Integrations · Missive
Your Privacy Choices
We and our vendors use technology that collects data about your use of our site so we can improve and personalize our products and services, for analytics and marketing, and to fulfill your requests. We may also share this information with marketing vendors, social media companies, and analytics partners. By using our website, you acknowledge and agree to our Terms of Use.
To learn more about our privacy practices review our
Privacy Policy
.

analysis.name: Missive
analysis.tagline: Tackle inboxes as a team, without chaos
analysis.value_proposition: Collaborative email client for teams to manage email, SMS, WhatsApp, and more in one shared inbox with internal comments, assignments, and rules.
analysis.ideal_customer: Customer support teams, sales teams, and agencies where email is mission-critical and team collaboration matters (accounting, law, real estate, logistics, e-commerce, travel).

features.name: team inboxes
features.description: Shared inboxes for handling email as a team

features.name: inline signature switcher
features.description: Change signature in composer without changing From address
features.evidence_quote: Choose a different signature without changing the From address

features.name: labels
features.description: Label conversations with support for nested labels

features.name: send later
features.description: Schedule emails to send at a later time

features.name: undo send
features.description: Undo an email send within a window

features.name: auto cc/bcc
features.description: Automatically add CC or BCC recipients

features.name: unlimited email import history
features.description: No cap on imported email history

features.description: Co-write email drafts in real-time with colleagues

features.description: See which teammates have read a conversation

differentiators: Collaborative email client built for teams (co-write drafts in real-time)
Unified inbox for email, SMS, WhatsApp, Instagram, Messenger, and live chat
Multi-provider AI (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) with BYOK support
First-class MCP integration (Stripe, Notion, Linear, custom servers)
Explicit positioning against Front, Gmelius, Help Scout, Hiver, Superhuman, Shortwave

recent_ships.what: Shareable label and mailbox links in comments
recent_ships.source: 11.30.1 June 19, 2026 — Share links to labels and mailboxes in comments
11.30.1 — new Signature button in top-right corner of signature area

recent_ships.what: Public REST API endpoint to update conversations without posting a comment
recent_ships.source: 11.30.1 — Update conversations through the API without posting a comment

recent_ships.what: Default meeting length preference for calendar events
recent_ships.source: 11.29.0 June 4, 2026 — Set your default meeting length

recent_ships.what: Improved shared address management (Google Groups, distribution lists)
recent_ships.source: 11.29.0 — Shared addresses now live in their own section
11.29.0 — Right-click any attachment to copy it straight to your clipboard

recent_ships.what: Smarter AI model selection (Fast vs Powerful) in rule actions
recent_ships.source: 11.29.0 — AI actions now show all available models in two clear categories

praised: Accessible to small teams — 'Your local car repair shop probably uses Missive'
G2 rating of 4.8 stars across 1000+ reviews
Positioned as 'accessible pricing' for non-Fortune companies
complaints: No substantive G2 or Hacker News feedback surfaced in provided sources — G2 page returned no content; HN mentions are unrelated (AMD Zen 2, historical references) or a single 5-upvote 'Improve your team email productivity with Missive' post

free_tier.limits: 30-day free trial + free plan for testing; no credit card required

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 5 users; email/SMS/social, team spaces, conversations & tasks, SOC 2 Type II

tiers.name: Productive
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 50 users; integrations, rules & automations, basic analytics, API access

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited users; SAML/SSO, IP restrictions, advanced analytics, personalized onboarding

pricing.notes: Pay-for-what-you-use pricing; 20% off annual billing; Starter capped at 5 users, Productive at 50; AI requires BYOK or Missive credits; 30-day free trial with no credit card. Pricing page cut off mid-FAQ about importing multiple accounts.
